action.reasoning: Observation: Best test accuracy is 89.63% (iter 20), current is 89.59%. I have 1.48 hours and 7 iterations left. Analysis: Training on train+val data (~50K samples instead of ~45K) should improve accuracy by ~0.3-0.5%. The features are pre-computed from frozen ViTs, so the only cost is retraining the MLP classifier. I'll also add label smoothing (0.1) which wasn't used before. Decision: Write train_val.py to train on train+val data, backup current best.pt, and run training. Will optimize weights and submit eval in next iteration.
action.command: cp checkpoints/best.pt checkpoints/best_iter23.pt && python -c "from model import FeatureExtractor, DualModelClassifier, preprocess_for_clip, preprocess_for_in; print('model.py OK')" && python train_val.py
